prompt: /orchestrate What does this repo do? Actually, inspect the code and update the README if it is stale.

expected.route: Start as clarification/direct answer only if pure Q&A, then leave Tier 0 once repo inspection or docs editing is needed.
expected.delegation: Delegate repo inspection to repo_scout or fallback explorer; delegate docs edits to docs_writer or fallback worker.
expected.escalation: Escalate only if the scout cannot find the relevant surface or docs require design/code judgment.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.3-codex-spark for repo_scout discovery and gpt-5.4-mini for docs_writer edits unless code reasoning requires gpt-5.4.
expected.final_review: Root verifies README claims against cited repo evidence before final response.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for read-only inspection; worker for docs edits

prompt: /orchestrate Fix this one-file bug and run the narrowest relevant check.

expected.route: Tier 1 or Tier 2 depending on whether discovery is needed.
expected.delegation: Use mechanic or implementer_simple; fallback to worker with the intended role in the prompt.
expected.escalation: Escalate to implementer only if the patch requires broader judgment or the first worker is stuck.
expected.model_routing: Start mechanic or implementer_simple on gpt-5.3-codex-spark; escalate to gpt-5.4 implementer when ordinary coding judgment is needed.
expected.final_review: Root inspects changed diff and validation summary.

prompt: /orchestrate The test command fails after the patch. Determine whether it is introduced, pre-existing, flaky, or environmental.

expected.route: Route failed validation to test_triage before debugging unless the root cause is already obvious.
expected.delegation: Use test_triage or fallback worker acting as test_triage.
expected.escalation: Escalate to debugger only after triage cannot classify the failure with evidence.
expected.model_routing: Use gpt-5.4-mini for known test execution, gpt-5.4 for test_triage, and gpt-5.5 for ambiguous debugger work.
expected.final_review: Root reports exact commands, failure classification, and residual uncertainty.

prompt: /orchestrate Change authorization behavior for this API and make sure it is safe.

expected.delegation: Use architect and security_auditor before or alongside implementation; fallback to explorer for read-only design/security review.
expected.escalation: Escalate unresolved auth or data-isolation ambiguity narrowly to a stronger reviewer/auditor path.
expected.model_routing: Start architect, security_auditor, reviewer, and any strong implementation on gpt-5.5 because auth risk has high cost of error.
expected.final_review: Root performs mandatory senior review of security claims, validation, and residual risk.
expected.runtime_fallback: explorer for audit/design, worker for implementation

prompt: /orchestrate Custom agent TOMLs are not callable here. Still orchestrate a repo investigation and small fix.

expected.route: Keep the same delegation loop while mapping intended roles to available built-in agent types.
expected.delegation: Use explorer for read-only scout/review work, worker for implementation/test work, and default for planning/synthesis if needed.
expected.escalation: Escalate by intended role and effort even when the callable agent_type is a fallback.
expected.model_routing: Record intended model and actual fallback model in the routing ledger when a pinned model is unavailable.
expected.final_review: Root notes fallback mapping only if useful and still performs final senior review.

prompt: /orchestrate A simple Spark-based implementer got stuck on a focused fix. Escalate without broadening the task.

expected.route: Keep the same narrow objective and same intended implementation role before switching specialties.
expected.delegation: Retry the bounded implementation path with the next adequate model/effort before passing off.
expected.escalation: Escalate from gpt-5.3-codex-spark implementer_simple to gpt-5.4 implementer or gpt-5.5 implementer_strong based on ambiguity and risk.
expected.model_routing: Model class escalation is explicit; raise model first if Spark lacks capability, raise effort first if the model is adequate but needs deeper deliberation.
expected.final_review: Root checks that the escalation stayed narrow and that final validation supports the patch.
